description: Cinematic explosion VFX on fatal collision game over — fire/debris particles, shockwave rings, screen flash, traffic scatter, and gradual fade-in to game over screen

userStories.title: Create explosionState FSM state and wire fatal collision to it
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dedicated explosionState in the FSM to orchestrate the cinematic explosion sequence, keeping it cleanly separated from gameOverState logic.
acceptanceCriteria: New explosionState object with onEnter(), update(dt), render(ctx) methods added to src/main.js
In handleVehicleCollision(), fatal collision (scrollSpeed > 600) transitions to explosionState instead of gameOverState
Remove the old spawnExplosion() call from handleVehicleCollision() for fatal collisions
explosionState.onEnter() captures explosion origin (player car center x/y), saves final stats (elapsedTime, distanceTraveled, score), and initializes an explosion timer to 2.0s
explosionState.onEnter() sets a player car visibility flag to false so the car is hidden during explosion
explosionState.render() calls renderRoad(), renderTraffic() to show the frozen game world as background (no position updates)
explosionState.render() does NOT call renderPlayer() (car is hidden)
explosionState.render() does NOT render collectible items (fuel, coins, nitro, shields) during explosion
explosionState.update() decrements the explosion timer; when timer reaches 0, transitions to gameOverState
gameOverState receives final stats from explosionState (finalTime, finalDistance, finalScore) — no stat capture logic duplicated in gameOverState.onEnter() for collision deaths
Fuel empty death still transitions directly to gameOverState (skips explosionState), and gameOverState.onEnter() still captures stats for fuel empty deaths
Typecheck/lint passes

userStories.title: Fire and debris particle burst on explosion
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see fire and debris particles burst outward from the car on fatal collision so that the crash feels dramatic and visceral.
acceptanceCriteria: In explosionState.onEnter(), clear the existing particles[] array to remove any pre-existing particles (e.g. nitro trail blue particles)
Spawn 30-50 fire/debris particles from the explosion origin point
Fire particles use colors: #FFF176 (bright yellow), #FFB74D (orange), #FF7043 (red-orange), #E53935 (red)
Debris particles use colors: #757575 (gray), #4E342E (dark brown), mixed in at ~30% ratio
Particles spread outward in all directions with random velocities (200-400 px/s)
Particles have varied sizes (2-8px) and lifetimes (0.8-1.5s)
Particles fade out over their lifetime using the existing alpha = life/maxLife pattern
explosionState.update() calls updateParticles(dt) to tick particle positions and lifetimes
explosionState.render() calls renderParticles(ctx) after rendering the frozen world
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Reuse the existing particles[] array and updateParticles()/renderParticles() functions — same pipeline as sparks, just different colors/sizes/lifetimes. The spawnSparks() function can be used as reference but a new spawn function is needed for the different parameters.

userStories.title: Expanding shockwave rings replacing old explosion system
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see large expanding shockwave rings emanate from the crash point so that the explosion feels powerful and layered.
acceptanceCriteria: New shockwave ring system with its own data array (e.g. shockwaveRings[]) storing ring state
On explosion start, initialize 3-4 concentric rings from the explosion origin
Rings have staggered start times (e.g. 0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75 progress offsets) for cascading effect
Max radii are 120px, 140px, 160px, 180px (much larger than old 40-90px)
Ring colors shift from white/yellow (#FFFFFF, #FFF176) at small radius to orange/red (#FF7043, #E53935) as they expand
Rings fade in alpha as they expand (alpha = 1 - ringProgress)
Total ring animation duration ~1.2s
Ring line width starts at 4px and decreases as rings expand
explosionState.update() updates ring progress based on elapsed time
explosionState.render() renders rings after frozen world but before particles
Remove or deprecate old explosions[] array, spawnExplosion(), updateExplosions(), and renderExplosions() functions — they are fully replaced
Remove the updateExplosions(dt) call from gameOverState.update() and renderExplosions(ctx) call from gameOverState.render()
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: The old explosion system (explosions[] array at ~line 142, spawnExplosion at ~line 357, updateExplosions at ~line 361, renderExplosions at ~line 369) is fully replaced. gameOverState.update() calls updateExplosions(dt) at ~line 1921 and gameOverState.render() calls renderExplosions(ctx) at ~line 1934 — both must be removed.

userStories.title: Screen flash on impact
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a bright screen flash at the moment of collision so that the impact feels immediate and intense.
acceptanceCriteria: New screenFlashAlpha variable initialized to 1.0 in explosionState.onEnter()
explosionState.update() fades screenFlashAlpha from 1.0 to 0 over 0.2s (decrement by dt/0.2)
explosionState.render() draws a full-canvas white rectangle with globalAlpha = screenFlashAlpha after all other explosion effects
Flash is only visible for the first ~0.2s of the explosion, then fully transparent
Flash does not render when screenFlashAlpha <= 0
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Simple overlay effect. Render order in explosionState.render() should be: frozen world → shockwave rings → particles → screen flash (on top of everything briefly).

userStories.title: Nearby traffic reacts to explosion (scatter)
userStories.description: As a player, I want nearby traffic vehicles to react to the explosion so that the crash feels like it has real impact on the world.
acceptanceCriteria: In explosionState.onEnter(), mark traffic vehicles within ~120px of explosion center as scattered
Scattered vehicles get new properties: scatterVx, scatterVy (outward velocity), scatterRotation (spin rate), scatterAlpha (starts at 1.0)
Outward velocity is proportional to proximity — closer vehicles are pushed harder (e.g. 150-300 px/s)
Scatter direction is calculated as vector from explosion center to vehicle center
explosionState.update() updates scattered vehicles: position += velocity*dt, rotation += spinRate*dt, alpha decreases over time
Non-scattered vehicles remain frozen (no position/rotation updates)
Scattered vehicles fade out completely within ~1.5s
explosionState.render() renders scattered vehicles with their current rotation and alpha (use ctx.save/translate/rotate/restore)
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: renderTraffic() renders all vehicles normally (frozen). After that, scattered vehicles need an additional render pass with rotation/alpha applied. Alternatively, modify the render loop to check the scattered flag per vehicle. The traffic array already has vehicle objects with x, y, width, height, color properties.

userStories.title: Gradual game over screen fade-in over explosion
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game over screen to fade in gradually over the explosion so that the transition feels smooth and cinematic.
acceptanceCriteria: New fadeInAlpha variable tracked in explosionState, starts at 0
After explosion timer passes ~1.0s (60-70% of 2.0s total), fadeInAlpha begins increasing
fadeInAlpha increases from 0 to 1.0 over ~0.8-1.0s
explosionState.render() draws a dark overlay (fillStyle #0A0A1A with globalAlpha = fadeInAlpha) over everything
Game over text (GAME OVER title, stats, retry prompt) renders on top of the dark overlay with a separate textAlpha that starts increasing ~0.2s after fadeInAlpha starts
Game over text content matches existing gameOverState layout: GAME OVER in red 44px, stats in white 18px, score in bold 22px, best score, pulsing retry text
Enter key is blocked during explosionState — retry only works after transition to gameOverState
When explosionState transitions to gameOverState, the game over screen is already fully visible (no visual pop or flash)
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: The fade-in happens within explosionState's render, not in gameOverState. By the time we transition to gameOverState, the overlay is fully opaque and the text is fully visible, so the transition is seamless. gameOverState then takes over with its normal render (solid background + text). The stats (finalTime, finalDistance, finalScore, isNewBest, bestScore) must be computed in explosionState.onEnter() and shared with both the fade-in render and gameOverState.

userStories.title: Clean state reset on retry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all new explosion-related state to be properly cleaned up when the player retries, to prevent visual glitches or stale state in the next game.
acceptanceCriteria: resetGameState() clears particles[] array (already done, verify it still works)
resetGameState() clears the new shockwave rings array
resetGameState() resets player car visibility flag to true
resetGameState() resets screenFlashAlpha to 0
resetGameState() resets fadeInAlpha to 0
resetGameState() resets explosion timer to 0
Traffic array is already emptied in resetGameState() (gameState.traffic = []), which clears scattered flags — verify this
After retry from a collision death, the new game starts clean with no explosion artifacts visible
After retry from a fuel empty death, the new game starts clean as well
The old explosions[] array cleanup (explosions.length = 0) can be removed if the old system was fully replaced
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: resetGameState() is at ~line 544. It already clears particles.length = 0 and explosions.length = 0. Need to add cleanup for all new state variables introduced by previous stories. Also verify that retrying from explosionState (if Enter is somehow pressed) and from gameOverState both work correctly.
